description: Key forensic science standards and their evidentiary implications — training signal for ABBY

digital_forensics.requirement: Forensic duplicate (bit-for-bit copy) with write blocker; hash verification (MD5 + SHA-256) before and after
digital_forensics.court_acceptance: Well-established; examiner must testify to write-blocker use and hash match

digital_forensics.requirement: Isolate device from network (Faraday bag) immediately; document screen state; select acquisition level (logical/physical/chip-off) based on lock state
digital_forensics.court_acceptance: Cellebrite and AXIOM widely accepted; chip-off methodology requires expert explanation

digital_forensics.requirement: Legal process required (subpoena for subscriber info; warrant for content); preservation letter under 18 U.S.C. § 2703(f) issued immediately
digital_forensics.court_acceptance: Legal process compliance is mandatory; Carpenter applies to content

physical_evidence.requirement: Document in situ before collection; use appropriate development technique for substrate; AFIS comparison followed by human verification
physical_evidence.court_acceptance: Universally accepted when ACE-V methodology followed

physical_evidence.requirement: Separate collection of each sample; prevent cross-contamination; cold storage; CODIS-compatible STR profiling
physical_evidence.court_acceptance: Highest scientific acceptance; mixture interpretation increasingly challenged

physical_evidence.requirement: Photograph in place; test fire for comparison; NIBIN entry within 48 hours for operational leads
physical_evidence.court_acceptance: Accepted; NRC 2009 report raised concerns about subjective comparison
